76286954: > Hexo essentially handles the bulk of the process: "reading Markdown → calling marked → applying templates → writing HTML files" for you.
ab06ef1a: # How does Markdown "transform" into a webpage?
d4c130e2: Nowadays, many people use tools like [Hexo](https://hexo.io), [VuePress](https://vuepress.vuejs.org/), and [Docsify](https://docsify.js.org/#/) to build their own blogs or documentation sites. They only need to write `.md` files, type a few lines of commands, and eventually, they can see beautiful article pages in the browser.
6459753c: But what exactly happened in between?
cb841faa: The core part is: **parsing Markdown text into HTML**. In Hexo's default configuration, this task is handled by a library called **marked** (or rather, many themes/plugins use it).
8b0c59d5: > **marked is a Markdown parser and compiler written in JavaScript** that can convert Markdown strings into HTML strings.
cb2e5792:
- Pure JS, can be used in both Node.js environment and browser side
- Fast speed, wide community, mature ecosystem
- Supports custom rendering
6bbb4575: Then execute `node mdtext.js` in the terminal
177039e1: You will see output like this in the console
d3fa780f: Up to this point, you have experienced the role and core of marked
8212a179: > marked takes a Markdown string as input and outputs an Html string
fc23655f: ### 3 Using marked in a Web Page and Rendering
840a476a: The example above only "prints out HTML"; to actually display it in a browser, it needs to be combined with the DOM.
d1b562c0: If your project is still relatively simple and doesn't have a build tool, you can also directly use it via CDN:
6f502622: Create a new .html file and write in it
7819c446: Save, and open the HTML file, and you will be able to see it intuitively.
28bfccf7: After obtaining the Markdown text, call marked for parsing. Internally, marked performs two tasks.
8cf93142:
- Lexical Analysis (Lexer): Breaks down Markdown text into individual Tokens (headings, paragraphs, lists, code blocks, etc.)
- Syntax Analysis + Rendering (Parser + Renderer): Converts these Tokens into an HTML string.
39819d9d: After generating HTML on the Node side, Hexo writes the HTML into the final `.html` file; the browser inserts the HTML into the DOM, which is ultimately rendered by the browser.
d93d2f46: Often, blog themes want to have more detailed control over the HTML rendered from Markdown, such as
c3859d31: marked provides a mechanism called **Renderer**, which allows you to override certain "rendering rules".
8c66d67a: In this way, your headings will be rendered with anchor link structures, such as:
227db51a: In many themes of Hexo, similar custom processing is applied to code blocks, headings, images, and so on.
35f7667a: # Back to Hexo: How does Hexo use marked?
ecdc0752:
- Hexo will scan all `.md` files under `source/_post`
- For each file, it reads the content and parses the Front-matter (YAML header information, such as `title`, `date`, `tags`, etc.).
d1651443: - Hexo selects which "renderer plugin" to use based on the configuration;
75f54fa6: - The rendering plugin internally calls marked to convert the Markdown content of the main text into HTML.
34c1e4c1: 3. Fill the HTML into the theme template
7cc6880b:
- Hexo selects which "renderer plugin" to use based on the configuration;
- Hexo combines the article's HTML content and metadata (title, date, tags) into the final HTML structure.
5f7500ad: - All pages are ultimately written to the `public/` directory
5ea76ebe: - When deploying, simply upload the `public` directory to the server or hosting platform (such as Github Pages)
